_note: Single source of truth for the live site and the Studio editor. Website copy is English. Each item has: title, description (one line), detail (markdown, longer), links[], image.

profile.name: Stan Shih
profile.tagline: I find products I love — then build for them.
profile.subtagline: CS student who ships end-to-end across web, mobile, desktop, and browser extensions. AI-first by default.

about.short: Builder, inventor, and AI-first engineer. I make products end-to-end — and I'm happiest with something half-built on the screen in front of me.
paragraphs: I'm a CS student at NTNU. I was admitted to Carnegie Mellon's Information Systems program on Early Action — then stepped back from it. Walking away from a school I'd worked years to reach was the hardest thing I've done, and it taught me the lesson that runs through everything since: ability means little without discipline and ownership.

So I rebuilt, on my own terms. I went deep on the things I actually love — shipping products, and lately doing it with AI, which multiplied what a single person can make. I founded a community, created for an audience, and started turning ideas into working software the same week I have them.
The way I work now: see widely, lead with value, and help before I'm asked — opportunities tend to follow. I'd rather build something real and useful than talk about it.

principles.title: Ship end-to-end
principles.body: Web, mobile, desktop, extensions — whatever the product needs.

principles.title: AI-first
principles.body: Claude Code, MCP servers, and multi-agent workflows are part of how I build.

principles.title: Lead with value
principles.body: See widely, help first, and let opportunities follow.

principles.title: Own the outcome
principles.body: Discipline over talent. I finish what I start.

items.title: Colonist.io Stats Tracker
items.description: A fan-made Chrome MV3 overlay that reads Colonist's game log locally for live stats.
items.detail: Parses the in-page game log with a MutationObserver and reconstructs board state to show live dice distribution, each player's resources, and hidden-card uncertainty. Runs entirely in the browser — no data leaves the machine.

items.title: Course Checker
items.description: A production PWA that checks NTNU CS graduation credits against catalog rules.
items.detail: Loads a transcript and the department's catalog rules, then reports remaining required credits by category. Installable as a PWA and used by real students.

items.title: Antnest Chatbot
items.description: A production LINE + AI support system for a real dessert shop.
items.detail: Next.js + Supabase + Gemini, with admin tools and a reservation flow. Handles customer questions on LINE and hands off to staff when needed.

items.title: LINE Notify+
items.description: An Android app that reshapes LINE notifications for Nothing Phone users.
items.detail: Groups chat notifications, adds per-source settings, and ships with full Play Store assets. Built in Kotlin with Jetpack Compose.

items.title: ClaudePulse
items.description: A Windows tray monitor for multiple Claude Code sessions.
items.detail: Tracks permission waits, idle states, and activity across many concurrent Claude Code sessions via local hooks, surfaced in a lightweight .NET tray app.

items.title: Traffic Congestion Lab
items.description: An interactive canvas lab showing stop-and-go traffic waves.
items.detail: Simulates how tiny braking disturbances cascade into stop-and-go congestion waves on a three-lane stadium track. Built with React and Canvas.

items.title: English Companion
items.description: An iPad PWA workflow for daily reading and writing practice.
items.detail: Models a learner over time and gives Claude Code-assisted feedback on daily reading and writing. Next.js PWA backed by Drizzle + Postgres.

items.title: ETF / Quant Research
items.description: A research codebase for portfolio tracking and walk-forward quant experiments.
items.detail: Walk-forward backtesting with explicit bias and overfit controls (deflated Sharpe). A place to test ideas honestly before trusting them.

items.title: Autonomous Financial API Discovery
items.description: An AI-agent pipeline that probes exchange APIs for undocumented behavior.
items.detail: Uses autonomous research agents to surface behavior that isn't in the docs — real rate limits, degradation under extreme conditions, and cross-endpoint quota sharing.

patent.title: Travel Smart Collision-Avoidance Warning System
patent.blurb: A vehicle-to-vehicle collision-warning system I invented in high school, inspired by my dad's Tesla. Granted dual patents in the US and Taiwan.
highlights: Cited by GM Cruise, Huawei, and Plus.ai in autonomous-driving patents
Gold medal, Ukraine International Invention Expo; Silver, Poland — youngest awardee
International Outstanding Inventor Award; received by the President of Taiwan

points: Independently sourced an overlooked startup deal (~$30M valuation), demonstrating market judgment and independent analysis.
Found the user onboarding bottleneck and shipped a fix that cut activation time by 30%.
Handled 160+ support tickets, kept VIP customers satisfied, and helped build $10K ARR.
Grew the community 50% year over year.
Partnered with creators for 220K+ views and presented to 40+ media outlets at a phone launch — likely the only university student ever to present at one.
Created sponsored content for brands including Nvidia; one post ranked top-5 by views among platform business collaborations.

education.note: On leave. Also admitted to Purdue Engineering and UW–Madison CS.
Mayor's Award (middle school), Speaker's Award (high school).
